summary: Implemented five scratch items resolved by PR body links #2550 through #2554 across camera, Flatzone, DevText, and particle code. The diff is mostly source-shaping for decomp matching: local aliases, scoped dont_inline pragmas, stack padding, byte masking, and equivalent control-flow rewrites. The decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 "No changes", supporting that these were matching/source reconstruction changes rather than intentional game behavior changes.

key_files.role: Reshaped gmCamera_801A30E4 with local struct and field pointer aliases plus a comma expression around gcus->x8.
Simplified grFlatzone_80218260 scope/control flow by hoisting HSD_JObj declaration and using assignment in the NULL check.
Added scoped dont_inline pragmas to DevText_InitPool and DevText_CreateCObj; added PAD_STACK(8) and masked render priority in DevText_Setup.
Added scoped dont_inline pragmas to small DevText flag/background/text/erase helpers.
Rewrote hsd_8039CEAC to index hsd_804D0908 directly instead of walking a bins pointer.

what_changed: gmCamera_801A30E4 now introduces gmCameraUnkStruct* gcus, s32* pxc, and HSD_JObj** px8 to access gmCamera_80479BC8.gcus fields through locals instead of repeatedly spelling the global field chain.
gmCamera_801A30E4 now uses *pxc for gmCamera_803DA6B4 indexing, gcus->x4 for JOBJ_HIDDEN flag toggles, *px8 for lb_80011E24, and HSD_JObjSetFlagsAll((0, gcus->x8), JOBJ_HIDDEN).
grFlatzone_80218260 now declares HSD_JObj* jobj before setting gp->gv.flatzone.xC7 and uses if ((jobj = GET_JOBJ(gobj)) != NULL) before hiding the JObj.

DevText_InitPool, DevText_CreateCObj, DevText_ShowBackground, DevText_HideBackground, DevText_ShowText, DevText_HideText, and DevText_Erase were wrapped in #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop.
DevText_Setup gained PAD_STACK(8) and passes devtext_setup_render_priority & 0xFF to GObj_SetupGXLink.
hsd_8039CEAC removed the void** bins temporary and bins++ loop increment, replacing *bins tests and loads with hsd_804D0908[i].
smart_moves: Scoped the dont_inline matching tactic with #pragma push/pop so only the targeted small DevText functions are affected.
Used local aliases and pointer-to-field temporaries in gmCamera_801A30E4 to tune code generation without changing apparent behavior.
Applied a narrow & 0xFF mask at the GObj_SetupGXLink call site, a precise way to force byte-width behavior for a render priority argument.
Used PAD_STACK(8) only in DevText_Setup, indicating a localized stack-layout match rather than broader structural churn.
Changed hsd_8039CEAC from pointer iteration to array indexing, showing that equivalent loop forms can be chosen based on the compiler output needed.
Kept unknown names such as gmCameraUnkStruct fields x4/x8/xC/x14 and hsd_8039CEAC rather than over-renaming without evidence.
decomp_lessons: When behavior is already understood but matching is blocked, equivalent C rewrites such as local aliases, indexed arrays, assignment-in-condition, and declaration hoisting can be decisive.
Small helper functions may need explicit dont_inline pragmas to preserve original function boundaries; use push/pop to avoid leaking the pragma state.
Stack padding via PAD_STACK should be applied narrowly and documented by the diff context because it is a codegen tactic, not business logic.
If a callee or original code path treats an int-like value as a byte, an explicit & 0xFF at the call site may match the original narrowing.
Do not assume pointer-walk C is closer to original assembly than indexed array C; compare generated code and choose the form that matches.
A decomp.dev "No changes" report is useful evidence that source-shaping changes preserved the GALE01 output.
naming_conventions: Camera code retained the gmCamera_ prefix and unknown-field style, including gmCamera_801A30E4, gmCamera_80479BC8.gcus, gmCamera_803DA6B4, and fields x4, x8, xC, x14.
The new local name gcus abbreviates gmCameraUnkStruct and pxc/px8 indicate pointer-to-field temporaries; evidence is limited to this diff and should not be generalized too far.
Dev text functions use the DevText_ prefix with descriptive PascalCase suffixes such as DevText_ShowBackground, DevText_HideText, and DevText_Erase.
Flatzone stage functions keep the grFlatzone_ address-style naming convention, e.g. grFlatzone_80218260 and grFlatzone_802182B4.
SysDolphin baselib particle functions and globals remain address-named where semantics are still unknown, e.g. hsd_8039930C, hsd_8039CEAC, and hsd_804D0908.
HSD object and graphics types/macros follow existing uppercase/type-prefix conventions: HSD_JObj, HSD_GObj, GXColor, GET_GROUND, GET_JOBJ, JOBJ_HIDDEN.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop around targeted functions
Pointer-to-field temporaries such as s32* pxc = &gcus->xC and HSD_JObj** px8
Assignment expression in condition: if ((jobj = GET_JOBJ(gobj)) != NULL)
Array indexing hsd_804D0908[i] instead of incrementing a void** bins pointer
Local alias for a global substructure: gmCameraUnkStruct* gcus = &gmCamera_80479BC8.gcus
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews were captured in the PR slice.
PR body lists completed resolutions for #2550, #2551, #2552, #2553, and #2554.
decomp.dev bot comment reported: "Report for GALE01 ... No changes".
follow_up_queries: gmCamera_801A30E4 gcus pxc px8 comma expression matching
hsd_8039CEAC hsd_804D0908 indexed array bins pointer
